entries.title: SpaceX has an AI device prototype, and it sure sounds phone-ish
entries.description: SpaceX reportedly showed investors a "handset-like" AI device before going public. It could be another signal SpaceX wants to expand into wireless.

entries.title: Ashton Kutcher leaving Sound Ventures to launch new VC firm with Morgan Beller

entries.title: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
entries.description: Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.

entries.title: Venice AI becomes a unicorn with $65M Series A as its privacy-first AI platform takes off
entries.description: Venice AI is already profitable, with annualized run-rate revenues of over $70 million, CEO Erik Voorhees said.

entries.title: Gemini Spark, Google’s agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
entries.description: Google's 24/7 agentic assistant, Gemini Spark, comes to Mac alongside other improvements, like real-time tracking and support for more apps.

entries.title: Builders Stage agenda revealed: Practical strategies for scaling startups at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026

entries.title: Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash

entries.title: The ‘Father of the Internet’ is finally retiring
entries.description: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.

entries.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
entries.description: The Trump administration's erratic approach to AI policymaking has left companies across the industry with little clarity about what will govern future model releases.

entries.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
entries.description: Wayve’s offering is part of a growing trend of AI startups using employee tenders as a strategic tool to attract and retain talent.

entries.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
entries.description: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

entries.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
entries.description: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.

entries.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
entries.description: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

entries.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
entries.description: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

entries.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund

entries.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
entries.description: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

entries.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now

entries.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
